summary: Iteration 4 — Backend Phase 1 (Deposits) + Phase 2 (Packs & VIP) end-to-end testing + frontend Economy smoke validation. Created /app/backend/tests/test_ton_economy_phase12.py (20 tests, all passing). Verified deposit init/status/history, balance (zero-default + after manual credit), pack & VIP catalogs, pack buy (start one-time, xp stackable extension semantics, insufficient balance 400, unknown pack 404, already-purchased 400), VIP buy (0→3 cumulative price 10.4 TON with [1,2,3] purchased & benefits match vip_benefits(3), target<=current 400, insufficient 400, target=31 & target=0 → 422), and audit logs persisted to pack_purchases / vip_purchases collections. Frontend smoke: ?guest=1 loads cleanly with NO console errors; tab-economy reveals economy-shop with Packs tab default showing both pack cards & bilingual copy & correct prices (5 TON / 1.5 TON); economy-tab-vip shows vip-row-1..30, vip-buy buttons, current-vip-level='Lv 0', economy-balance='0.0000 TON'.

minor.issue: expires_in_sec returned as 1800s (30min) but _expire_old_deposits() uses a 1h cutoff — the client-visible expiry and the server-side reap window are inconsistent. Document or align (likely raise expires_in_sec to 3600 or lower the reap cutoff).

one_time check uses player.start_pack_purchased regardless of pack_id, so if another future one_time pack is added it will incorrectly share the start_pack_purchased flag. Use a per-pack purchased flag (e.g., purchased_packs: List[str]).
Non-existent wallet returns 200 with zeros (per spec), but does NOT persist a player doc. Consistent but worth noting since /api/player/{wallet} GET DOES auto-create — UX asymmetry.

vip_purchased_levels stored as a Python list and extended in-process — race-safe only because pymongo $set replaces; concurrent purchases for the same wallet could lose levels. Consider $push with $each + transactional balance check.

issues: VIP row layout cramped: benefit chips (+2% XP, +1% Gold, +1% DMG, 10% tax) wrap awkwardly around the price label '1.6 TON' on a 1280px viewport. Consider a grid: [Lv badge | benefits column | price | CTA] with consistent column widths.

action_items: Align deposit init expires_in_sec (1800s) with _expire_old_deposits cutoff (1h) — pick one source of truth.
Refactor one_time pack tracking to per-pack flag to support future one-time packs.
Polish VIP row layout in EconomyShop.jsx (grid columns) so benefits don't wrap around the price.
critical_code_review_comments: VIP & pack purchases are not atomic: balance check + $inc are separate read/write operations. A racing double-click can debit twice. Consider a conditional update like update_one({wallet, ton_balance: {$gte: price}}, {$inc: {ton_balance: -price}}) and check modified_count.
TREASURY_WALLET is read at import time; if rotated, requires a service restart. Acceptable but document it.
_extend_xp_pack tolerates malformed ISO timestamps by falling back to now() — silently masks data corruption. Add a logger.warning when fallback triggers.

Pack/VIP buy endpoints do not require auth (no Telegram initData check). Anyone who knows a wallet string can purchase on its behalf — but since ton_balance can only be credited by real on-chain deposits to TREASURY_WALLET with the wallet's comment, the attack surface is limited to spending another user's confirmed balance. Worth gating with initData or a session token in Phase 3.
Audit-log collections (pack_purchases, vip_purchases) lack indexes on wallet + created_at. Add for query perf as volume grows.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (smoke: economy tab, packs tab, vip tab all render with correct testids and zero console errors)

test_credentials: No auth used. Test wallets generated as TEST_<category>_<uuid8>. Mongo credentials read from /app/backend/.env (DB_NAME=chronicles_of_ton).
seed_data_creation: Test wallets credited directly via Mongo with $set:{ton_balance:N} after API auto-creates the player doc (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md procedure). All TEST_* wallets cleaned up in module-scope teardown across collections: players, deposits, pack_purchases, vip_purchases.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1+2 backend is solid. For Phase 3+ (withdrawals, admin panel), reuse the helper pattern: _credit(mongo, wallet, amount) which auto-creates the player via GET /api/player/{wallet} then directly updates ton_balance. Audit log collections to inspect: pack_purchases, vip_purchases (both indexed by wallet+created_at recommended). Frontend test IDs verified working: tab-economy, economy-shop, economy-tab-packs, economy-tab-vip, pack-card-start, pack-card-xp, pack-buy-start, pack-buy-xp, vip-row-N (N=1..30), vip-buy-N, economy-balance, current-vip-level.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found this iteration. All Phase 1+2 acceptance criteria from the review request pass.
